issue_title: [Refactor] Timestamp links in comments open external browser instead of seeking video

reasoning_trace.finding: The local checkout contains TimestampExtractor, TimestampLongPressClickableSpan, and TextLinkifier timestamp handling, but no DescriptionText.kt or Compose comment component under app/src/main/java.
No Compose LinkAnnotation/UrlAnnotation/ClickableText implementation was found in the local source tree; comment UI appears to be the classic RecyclerView/TextView implementation.

CommentsFragment is the entry fragment for comments. It loads comment data through ExtractorHelper.getCommentsInfo/getMoreCommentItems and uses the list adapter path; it does not render or handle link clicks itself.

CommentInfoItemHolder binds each CommentsInfoItem. Lines 128-130 set streaming service, stream URL, and comment Description into TextEllipsizer. Lines 134-153 manually dispatch ClickableSpan clicks inside the comment TextView.

Comment text flows from item.getCommentText() into TextEllipsizer after the related service and stream URL have been set, so the classic comment path has enough context to create timestamp seek spans.

TextEllipsizer.linkifyContentView calls TextLinkifier.fromDescription(view, content, HtmlCompat.FROM_HTML_MODE_LEGACY, streamingService, streamUrl, disposable, ...). This delegates comment text linkification to the shared TextLinkifier.

TextLinkifier.fromDescription routes HTML, Markdown, or plain text into changeLinkIntents. changeLinkIntents replaces URLSpan with UrlLongPressClickableSpan and, when relatedInfoService and relatedStreamUrl are available, calls addClickListenersOnTimestamps.

The timestamp-specific branch is guarded by relatedInfoService != null and relatedStreamUrl != null. In the classic comment holder those values are supplied, so plain timestamps should receive TimestampLongPressClickableSpan.
TIMESTAMPS_PATTERN detects plain timestamps like MM:SS and HH:MM:SS, and getTimestampFromMatcher converts them into seconds.

TimestampLongPressClickableSpan.onClick calls InternalUrlsHandler.playOnPopup(context, relatedStreamUrl, relatedInfoService, timestampMatchDTO.seconds()), which is the expected internal player seek behavior for timestamp text.
The video description path also uses TextLinkifier.fromDescription with getService() and getStreamUrl(), confirming this shared utility is the intended timestamp handling path.
The comment text view is itemCommentContentView, a NewPipeTextView inside a clickable RelativeLayout. The holder explicitly handles link touch events on this TextView.
No Compose UI source matching the bug report's named DescriptionText.kt/Comment.kt path exists in this checkout, eliminating a direct local file candidate for AnnotatedString.fromHtml.

Generic URL clicks call InternalUrlsHandler.handleUrlDescriptionTimestamp(context, url), and if that returns false, line 26 calls ShareUtils.openUrlInApp(context, url), which opens the URL through Android's external app resolution.

handleUrlDescriptionTimestamp only handles URLs matching (.*)&t=(\d+). If a comment timestamp is represented only as a generic URL annotation and this URL handler does not recognize it, the click falls through to the external app path.

The row click toggles expansion, but the comment TextView onTouchListener consumes taps that intersect ClickableSpan ranges first. This is not the most likely direct cause of external browser opening in the local code path.
This method implements the desired timestamp seek behavior and is reached when service and stream URL context are supplied, so it is not the faulty statement for external browser opening.

reasoning_trace.target: Generic URL fallback is the local statement triggering external browser/app opening
reasoning_trace.finding: Given the local checkout lacks the reported Compose source, the closest exact local source statement causing the observed symptom is the fallback ShareUtils.openUrlInApp call in UrlLongPressClickableSpan.onClick after timestamp interception fails.

fault_candidates.reasoning: This is the exact local statement that opens a clicked comment/description URL via Android ACTION_VIEW when NewPipe does not recognize the URL as an internal timestamp link. The bug report's Compose files are absent from this checkout, but the reported symptom, timestamp treated as generic web link and opened externally, is produced by this fallback path.

final_answer.causal_chain: A timestamp in comment text is represented as a generic URL span rather than a TimestampLongPressClickableSpan. CommentInfoItemHolder dispatches the ClickableSpan click from the comment TextView. UrlLongPressClickableSpan.onClick first asks InternalUrlsHandler.handleUrlDescriptionTimestamp(context, url) to intercept timestamp URLs. If that handler returns false, execution reaches ShareUtils.openUrlInApp(context, url), which creates an ACTION_VIEW URL intent and lets Android open the browser or YouTube app instead of seeking the in-app player.
final_answer.fix_hint: Ensure comment timestamp links are converted to a timestamp-specific handler before the generic URL fallback, or extend the URL timestamp interception so recognized comment timestamp URLs call InternalUrlsHandler.playOnPopup/getPlayerTimestampIntent instead of ShareUtils.openUrlInApp.
